title: Variables and Data Types
objectives: Create and assign variables in Python
Understand the difference between strings, integers, floats, and booleans
Use type() to inspect data types
Convert between data types with int(), str(), float()
hints: Variables are created with the = operator: name = 'Alex'
Python has four basic types: str, int, float, bool. Use type(x) to check.
To convert types: int('42') gives 42, str(42) gives '42'
assessmentCriteria: All variables are assigned with correct types
Type conversions produce the expected values
Program runs without TypeError or ValueError
